final_embedding_text:
Context: This JLL report, published in 2024, focuses on land transactions in India. This specific section provides a snapshot of key land transaction metrics for the year 2023, including area acquired, value, development potential, and the number of deals.

Chunk Content: Land transactions in India

# Land transactions: Snapshot 2023

| Metric | Value |
| :--- | :--- |
| Area of land acquired by real estate developers | 1,947 acres |
| Value of land acquired by real estate developers | INR 32,203 crore |
| Development potential of land acquired | 176 mn sqft |
| Number of deals | 111 deals |
| % of land acquired for proposed residential developments | 70% |

**Note:** Joint Development Agreements (JDAs) are not included for the analysis. Only outright purchases by real estate developers are considered for the study. The development potential is estimated based on the permissible FAR/FSI allowed in the respective micro markets where the land has been transacted. Sales potential is estimated considering the loading component of the development potential area and the average saleable price in the respective micro markets where the land has been transacted.

Context: This JLL report, published in 2024, analyzes land transactions across India, focusing on acquisitions by real estate developers. This specific section details the year-over-year increase in land area and value acquired by developers in 2023 compared to 2022, along with the associated development potential.

Chunk Content: Land transactions in India

# 2023: 21% y-o-y increase in land acquired by real estate developers

| Year | Land acquired (Acres) | Potential development size (Million Sqft) |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| 2022 | 1,603 | 150 |
| 2023 | 1,947 | 176 |

**Caption:** Land acquired by real estate developers increased to 1,947 acres (Up by 21% y-o-y). Units: Acres and Million Sqft.

| Year | Value of land acquired (INR crore) |
| :--- | :--- |
| 2022 | 18,112 |
| 2023 | 32,203 |

**Caption:** 78% y-o-y increase in value of land acquired by real estate developers. Units: INR crore.

* As the real estate sector is on a steep growth curve, developers are building a robust supply pipeline by investing in land acquisitions across the country.
* A total of 1,947 acres of land valued at INR 32,203 crore was acquired by real estate developers in 2023 as compared to 1,603 acres in 2022 (valued at INR 18,112 crore), up by 21% y-o-y.
* The land acquired in 2023 had a development potential of ~176 million sqft.

Context: This JLL report, published in 2024, provides an annual review of land transactions across India, focusing on data from 2023. This specific section details the year-on-year increase in the number of land deals and the average transacted value per acre for 2022 and 2023.

Chunk Content: Land transactions in India

# 2023: Per acre transacted value of land rose by 46% y-o-y

| Year | Number of deals |
| :--- | :--- |
| 2022 | 64 |
| 2023 | 111 |

**Caption:** Number of deals increased by 1.73X in 2023.

| Year | No. of deals with transacted land value of INR 100 crore per acre or above |
| :--- | :--- |
| 2022 | 10 |
| 2023 | 13 |

**Caption:** Growth in high-value land deals (INR 100 crore per acre or above).

| Year | Average value of land transacted (INR Crore per acre) |
| :--- | :--- |
| 2022 | 11.3 |
| 2023 | 16.5 |

**Caption:** Average value of land transacted reached INR 16.5 crore per acre (Up by 46% y-o-y). Units: INR Crore.

* Number of land transactions increased from 64 in 2022 to 111 in 2023.
* **Average value of land transacted touched INR 16.5 crore per acre in 2023, up from INR 11.3 crore in 2022.**
* Number of deals with transacted land value of INR 100 crore per acre or above gone up from 10 deals in 2022 to 13 deals in 2023.

final_embedding_text:
Context: This JLL report, published in 2024, analyzes land transactions across India in 2023. This specific section details the city-wise breakup of land area acquired by real estate developers, highlighting Delhi NCR's lead in both area and number of deals.

Chunk Content: Land transactions in India

# Delhi NCR led in terms of land area acquired in 2023

| City | % Share of land area acquired |
| :--- | :--- |
| Delhi NCR | 21% |
| Ludhiana | 16% |
| Bengaluru | 16% |
| MMR | 15% |
| Chennai | 11% |
| Nagpur | 8% |
| Hyderabad | 4% |
| Others | 4% |
| Pune | 2% |
| Ahmedabad | 2% |
| Kolkata | 1% |

**Caption:** 53% of the land was acquired in Delhi NCR, Ludhiana and Bengaluru (Total ~1,947 acres).

| City | Land Acquired (Acres) |
| :--- | :--- |
| Delhi NCR | 414.8 |
| Ludhiana | 320.0 |
| Bengaluru | 304.5 |
| MMR | 288.9 |
| Chennai | 208.5 |
| Nagpur | 167.0 |
| Hyderabad | 71.5 |
| Others | 73.4 |
| Pune | 47.4 |
| Ahmedabad | 37.6 |
| Kolkata | 12.9 |

**Caption:** More than 1,000 acres of land acquired in Delhi NCR, Ludhiana and Bengaluru. Units: Acres.

* In 2023, **Delhi NCR led both in terms of number of land deals and area acquired with ~415 acres across 36 separate land deals** valued at INR 9,120 crore.
* Select established players acquired multiple land parcels across Delhi NCR.
* Out of these 415 acres, 264 acres (64%) valued over INR 5,300 crore was acquired in Gurugram alone. This was followed by Noida with over 59 acres (14%) land acquired valued at INR 1,775 crore. Delhi, Faridabad and Sonipat contributed the rest.
* **Ludhiana had a share of 16% in land area acquired** as a large transaction for residential development was concluded there.

*Others include Sanand, Ayodhya and Dholera*

* Delhi NCR led in terms of number of deals with a total of 36 deals locked in 2023, followed by MMR with 24 deals.

Context: JLL's 2024 report on land transactions in India details the proposed development on land acquired in 2023. This section specifically highlights that the residential segment accounted for 70% of the 1,947 acres of land acquired, with a potential for 130 million sqft of residential space.

Chunk Content: Land transactions in India

# 03
# Land transactions: Proposed development



Land transactions in India

# Residential segment led the way in land transactions locked in 2023

### Proposed development on the acquired land: 1,947 acres

| Segment | Share (%) |
| :--- | :--- |
| Residential | 70% |
| Industrial & Warehousing | 20% |
| Office | 6% |
| Retail | 3% |
| Data centre | 1% |

**Caption:** Distribution of proposed development on 1,947 acres of acquired land in 2023 by sector.

***

*   **1,365 acres** acquired for proposed residential developments
*   **130 million sqft** of residential space to be developed
*   **Topline of INR 1,38,750 crore** to be achieved from the sale of these residential projects

***

*   Out of the 1,947 acres acquired in 2023, around 1,365 acres (~70%) was acquired for proposed residential projects with a development potential of around 130 million sqft.
*   With a surge in demand for housing, developers are actively acquiring land parcels for building future supply pipeline.
*   2023 was a bonanza year for residential markets as sales were up by 26% y-o-y. Highest ever annual sales of 271,800 units were recorded in 2023, surpassing the peak seen in 2010 at 216,700 units, by 25%. Residential launches in 2023 witnessed a growth of 19% y-o-y.

11

contextual_situation: This section, titled "Outlook," is part of the JLL report "Land transactions in India" published in 2024. It serves as a forward-looking segment, discussing anticipated trends and expectations for land transactions across India.

final_embedding_text:
Context: This JLL report, published in 2024, provides an outlook on land transactions across India. It forecasts steady land transactions and strengthening residential project launches in 2024, driven by factors like interest rate moderation, housing demand, and infrastructure development, including in Tier 2 and 3 cities.

Chunk Content: Land transactions in India

Going forward in 2024, we expect **land transactions to remain steady** with developers expanding their land banks on the back of expected moderation in interest rates, growing demand for housing and support from institutional funding agencies.

Infrastructure changes, specially enhanced metro connectivity between various micro-markets, will throw up **new pockets of growth within each city.**

The **launches of new residential projects are expected to strengthen further** through new land acquisitions in strategic locations and growth corridors.

Strategic land acquisitions by real estate developers in **tier 2 and 3 cities** is expected to continue to leverage the rising demand for quality projects.

13
raw_text:
Land transactions in India

The **launches of new residential projects are expected to strengthen further** through new land acquisitions in strategic locations and growth corridors.

Strategic land acquisitions by real estate developers in **tier 2 and 3 cities** is expected to continue to leverage the rising demand for quality projects.

### About JLL
For over 200 years, JLL (NYSE: JLL), a leading global commercial real estate and investment management company, has helped clients buy, build, occupy, manage and invest in a variety of commercial, industrial, hotel, residential and retail properties. A Fortune 500® company with annual revenue of $20.8 billion and operations in over 80 countries around the world, our more than 106,000 employees bring the power of a global platform combined with local expertise. Driven by our purpose to shape the future of real estate for a better world, we help our clients, people and communities SEE A BRIGHTER WAY<sup>SM</sup>. JLL is the brand name, and a registered trademark, of Jones Lang LaSalle Incorporated. For further information, visit jll.com

This report has been prepared solely for information purposes and does not necessarily purport to be a complete analysis of the topics discussed, which are inherently unpredictable. It has been based on sources we believe to be reliable, but we have not independently verified those sources and we do not guarantee that the information in the report is accurate or complete. Any views expressed in the report reflect our judgment at this date and are subject to change without notice. Statements that are forward-looking involve known and unknown risks and uncertainties that may cause future realities to be materially different from those implied by such forward-looking statements. Advice we give to clients in particular situations may differ from the views expressed in this report. No investment or other business decisions should be made based solely on the views expressed in this report.
